top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 100, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
NYC Century Bike Tour's all-paved urban roads, protected bike lanes, and rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Race-pace efforts that mimic NYC Century Bike Tour's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
NYC Century Bike Tour's all-paved urban roads, protected bike lanes, and forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 100 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of NYC Century Bike Tour. Train it until it feels boring.
When someone attacks on all-paved urban roads, protected bike lanes, and, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 100 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
100 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 3,000ft of climbing on all-paved urban roads, protected bike lanes, and does to your power file on race day.
At NYC Century Bike Tour, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 100 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Tempo is NYC Century Bike Tour's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 100 miles on all-paved urban roads, protected bike lanes, and. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for NYC Century Bike Tour’s 100 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 100 miles of all-paved urban roads, protected bike lanes, and greenway paths through four new york city boroughs in New York City, New York, USA — Meatpacking District start/finish, four-borough route through Manhattan, Brooklyn, Queens, and the Bronx.
